title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. forondae
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. forondae, commonly known as Kidney Vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Kidney Vetch include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Kidney Vetch can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
